motto: Serving Greywater Falls since 1887, and the lake somewhat longer.
weather: 28°F, first hard frost of the season. Clear sky, absolutely clear, no clouds, no wind. The lake is a mirror. A thin white rind of ice at the outermost pilings, exactly where Agnes said it would begin.

undercurrent.note: The dock has begun to freeze from the outside in, precisely as Agnes Crewe stated in print. The prediction is now on the dock itself, confirmed by two witnesses before eight a.m. The town has crossed from 'something is happening' into 'something is arriving.' Russ opened a file; this is the constabulary's first official acknowledgment. Agnes told Wren to set out the good cups, which is the most direct instruction she has given. The box holds ten items — one over census — and no new envelope today, which is its own kind of pressure: the correspondence has said what it needed to say. The next beat is the middle planks freezing (around the 11th), the shore end on the 14th, and then D. Harrow arrives or the lake does something that constitutes an arrival. Doreen's turnovers carry the same pattern as the blue border: the town is beginning to rhyme with itself. Toby put his phone away, which means even the observer has passed the threshold of what documentation can hold.

editor.name: Wren Aldercott
editor.voice: Dry, precise, allergic to drama, which is unfortunate given recent events. Writes like a person holding a clipboard while standing in rising water. Lets one true thing slip per edition, usually in the last line.

season_arc.premise: Something is using the Gazette's lost-and-found as a postal address — and the letters it sends back are addressed to people who have not yet arrived in Greywater Falls.
